description: ZKSettle Landing + Dashboard for Colosseum Frontier 2026 — editorial marketing surface and read-only dashboard aligned with the canonical design system spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-zksettle-landing-design.md and implementation plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-18-zksettle-landing.md. Stack: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui (token-customized), GSAP + ScrollTrigger, motion v11, Three.js vanilla, Shiki, Iconoir v7, pnpm 9. All work happens in /home/mario/zksettle/frontend/. Deadline: 2026-05-11.

userStories.title: Initialize Next.js 15 project with TypeScript strict
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a scaffolded Next.js 15 App Router project in frontend/ so the rest of the work has a home.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/package.json exists with Next 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind, App Router, src/ dir, @/* alias, pnpm, Turbopack
frontend/tsconfig.json has strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, noImplicitOverride: true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true
frontend/package.json scripts include: dev, build, start, typecheck, lint, test, test:watch
frontend/package.json has engines.node >=20.10 and packageManager pnpm@9.12.0
frontend/README.md exists with stack and pnpm dev instructions
pnpm install completes without error
pnpm build succeeds on the default scaffold
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scaffolded Next 15.5.15 (patched 15.x) + React 19.0.0 + Tailwind v4 + TS strict. Build and typecheck pass. next.config.ts pins outputFileTracingRoot to the frontend/ dir to silence the multi-lockfile warning from /home/mario.

userStories.title: Install ESLint flat config + Prettier + Vitest
userStories.description: As a developer, I need lint and test tooling before writing code so every subsequent task can verify cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Dev deps installed: eslint@9, eslint-config-next@15, typescript-eslint@8, prettier, prettier-plugin-tailwindcss, vitest@2, @vitest/ui, jsdom
frontend/eslint.config.mjs exists as flat config composing typescript-eslint and eslint-config-next
frontend/.prettierrc exists with semi: true, singleQuote: false, trailingComma: all, printWidth: 100, tailwind plugin
frontend/vitest.config.ts exists with node environment and @ alias pointing to src
pnpm lint exits 0
pnpm test exits 0 with no test files message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: eslint 9.39.4 + eslint-config-next 15.5.15 (consumed via FlatCompat from @eslint/eslintrc) + typescript-eslint 8.58.2 + prettier 3.8.3 + prettier-plugin-tailwindcss 0.7.2 + vitest 2.1.9 + @vitest/ui 2.1.9 + jsdom 29.0.2. Switched lint script from deprecated `next lint` to `eslint .`; added `--passWithNoTests` to the test script so vitest exits 0 on empty runs. Build still succeeds.

frontend/public/fonts/ directory exists with a .gitkeep
pnpm ls lists all listed packages at expected versions
pnpm build still succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Installed runtime deps: geist 1.7.0, iconoir-react 7.11.0, motion 12.38.0 (motion v11 deprecated; v12 is the current line and exposes the same React API), three 0.184.0, gsap 3.15.0, shiki 4.0.2, clsx 2.1.1, tailwind-merge 3.5.0, class-variance-authority 0.7.1. Dev: @types/three 0.184.0. public/fonts/.gitkeep created. Build, typecheck, lint, test all pass; landing first-load JS still 102 kB (deps tree-shake until imported).

userStories.title: Author design tokens in globals.css
userStories.description: As a designer, I need the full ZKSettle token system available via Tailwind v4 @theme so components can consume the canonical palette, typography, radii, and easing.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/globals.css imports tailwindcss and defines @theme block with all color tokens: canvas, surface, surface-deep, border-subtle, border, ink, quill, stone, muted, ghost, forest, forest-hover, emerald, mint, warning-bg/text, danger-bg/text, info-bg/text
Tokens also include font-display (Georgia), font-sans (Geist), font-mono (JetBrains/Berkeley fallback), radii 2/3/6/10, and --ease-brand cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1)
Global html/body rules apply canvas background, ink color, sans font family, font smoothing
em element rule: italic, forest color, inherit weight
::selection uses forest background with canvas text
prefers-reduced-motion media query disables animations
pnpm build succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Authored full @theme block in src/app/globals.css using Tailwind v4 naming (--color-*, --font-*, --radius-*, --ease-*). All 20 color tokens present (canvas/surface/surface-deep/border-subtle/border, ink/quill/stone/muted/ghost, forest/forest-hover/emerald/mint, warning|danger|info bg/text). Font tokens use next/font CSS variable fallbacks (--font-geist-sans, --font-jetbrains-mono) that US-005 will register, cascading to Georgia / system-ui / ui-monospace. Radii 2/3/6/10 + --ease-brand cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1). html/body apply canvas bg + ink + font-sans + antialiased smoothing. em rule forest italic inherit-weight. ::selection forest/canvas. prefers-reduced-motion disables animations + transitions + scroll-behavior. Build, typecheck, lint, test all pass.

userStories.title: Root layout with fonts, metadata, and skip link
userStories.description: As a user, I need every page to serve the correct fonts, SEO metadata, and a skip-to-content link.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/layout.tsx registers Geist Sans via next/font with --font-geist-sans CSS variable
JetBrains Mono (or Berkeley fallback) registered via next/font with --font-mono CSS variable
Metadata object defines title default + template, description, openGraph (title, description, type website, images placeholder), twitter card summary_large_image
html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning
body applies font variables to className and contains a skip-to-content anchor before children
pnpm dev renders / with Geist body copy and Georgia display headings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Registered Geist Sans via `geist/font/sans` (variable --font-geist-sans) and JetBrains Mono via `next/font/google` (variable --font-jetbrains-mono). Attached both font.variable classes on <html> so the CSS vars cascade globally; <body className="font-sans antialiased"> picks up the --font-sans token from globals.css. Metadata includes title.default + template, description, openGraph (title/description/type=website/images=/og), and twitter.card=summary_large_image. metadataBase resolves via NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL (defaults to http://localhost:3000) so relative OG image paths render as absolute URLs and silence Next's warning. Skip-to-content anchor lives as the first body child (sr-only by default, visible on focus with forest bg + canvas text + 2px forest outline); page.tsx now exposes the focus target via id="main-content" on <main>. Dev server verified: lang="en" present, both font variable classes on <html>, og:/twitter: meta tags render correctly, skip link visible in markup. Build, typecheck, lint, test all pass.

userStories.title: Build The Seal logo SVG component
userStories.description: As a brand, I need The Seal — circular forest ring with cancelled serif Z and registration ticks at 12/3/6/9 — as a reusable React component.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/components/icons/logo.tsx exports a Logo component with props: variant (canvas-ink | surface-forest | forest-surface), size (number, default 48), showWordmark (boolean, default true)
SVG contains: hairline ring 1.25px, serif Z centered, 2.5px horizontal settlement bar across Z midpoint, 4 registration ticks at 12/3/6/9
Wordmark (when shown) reads ZKSettle in Georgia serif at seal-diameter × 0.4 gap to the right
All three variants render correct fg/bg pairs from design tokens
Logo renders crisp at 16px (favicon) and 200px (hero)
Logo imported and rendered on landing nav so /
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Authored src/components/icons/logo.tsx exporting Logo({variant, size=48, showWordmark=true, className}) and internal Seal(). SVG uses viewBox 48x48, circle ring r=22 stroke=1.25, centered Georgia serif <text>Z</text> fontSize 26, 2.5px settlement bar (x 10.5→37.5 at y=24) rendered after the Z so it cancels the glyph, and 4 registration ticks (each 1.25px, 3px long) at 12/3/6/9 crossing the ring. Variants map to Tailwind token classes: canvas-ink → text-ink/text-ink, surface-forest → text-forest/text-ink, forest-surface → text-surface/text-surface. Seal uses currentColor so the variant class on the <svg> wrapper drives stroke+fill. Wordmark renders ZKSettle in font-display (Georgia) at size*0.5 font-size, gap between seal and wordmark = size*0.4 per spec §3.1 lockup. Aria: wordmark visible → span has no role; showWordmark=false → role=img aria-label=ZKSettle; the seal SVG is always aria-hidden. Imported into src/app/page.tsx as a temporary nav header (size 32, canvas-ink); US-011 will relocate to nav.tsx. Build, typecheck, lint, test all pass; dev server renders circle + ZKSettle wordmark at /.

userStories.title: Landing copy single source of truth
userStories.description: As a copywriter, I need all landing copy centralized in typed modules so iteration is one-file.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/content/copy.ts exports a COPY constant with entries for hero, paradox, twoRealities, howItWorks, numbers, demo, useCases, developers, momentum, closingCta, footer — each section matching the Design Spec §4 strings exactly
frontend/src/content/use-cases.ts exports a USE_CASES array of 5 items (Travel rule, Proof of solvency, ZK credit score, AML by behavior, Proof of reserves) with name, tagline, footnote
All exports are typed (no any)
Numbers block exposes {label, number, sub} tuples for each of <5s, <$0.001, 0, 256 bytes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Authored src/content/copy.ts exporting COPY: LandingCopy with 11 section entries (hero, paradox, twoRealities, howItWorks, numbers, demo, useCases, developers, momentum, closingCta, footer) — all strings lifted verbatim from Design Spec §4. Every shape declared as a readonly interface (HeroCopy, ParadoxCopy, TwoRealitiesCopy with TwoRealitiesRow/TwoRealitiesSide, HowItWorksCopy/HowItWorksStep, NumbersCopy/NumberTuple, DemoCopy/DemoFormField, UseCasesCopy, DevelopersCopy, MomentumCopy/MomentumColumn, ClosingCtaCopy, FooterCopy). Zero `any` — IDE inferred types for every leaf. Numbers.items is `readonly NumberTuple[]` of {label, number, sub}: <5s/"Proving time", <$0.001/"Verify cost", 0/"PII leaked", 256 bytes/"Proof size". TwoRealities rows include both plain + redacted values so US-015 can animate the dissolve without re-deriving ▓ strings. Demo form exposes field defaults (recipient 5g8H4nP3eR..., amount min 100/max 10000/default 1200, jurisdiction US default from options [US/EU/UK/BR]), generate/submit/expired toggle labels, honestyFooter + expiredError strings. Developers.code stores the 3-line SDK example as a multi-line string (joined from array for source clarity); Shiki will consume it in US-020. src/content/use-cases.ts exports USE_CASES: readonly UseCase[] of 5 items matching Design Spec §4 §7 lines exactly (AML by behavior has footnote: "" since spec line omits the third bullet). COPY.useCases.items re-exports USE_CASES so downstream section components can destructure from one import. Build, typecheck, lint, test all pass; bundle unchanged at 102 kB.

userStories.title: Utility lib — cn, format, seeded PRNG
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities (cn class merger, format helpers, deterministic PRNG) before building components that consume them.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/cn.ts exports cn() merging clsx + tailwind-merge
frontend/src/lib/format.ts exports truncateWallet(address, head=4, tail=4), fmtAmount(value, currency='USDC'), fmtCompact(n)
frontend/src/lib/prng.ts exports createPrng(seed) returning an object with next(), range(min,max), pick(array) using deterministic mulberry32 (or similar)
Vitest tests cover format and prng with at least 3 assertions each including determinism for prng
pnpm test passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Authored src/lib/cn.ts (clsx + tailwind-merge composition — the canonical shadcn pattern), src/lib/format.ts (truncateWallet uses … ellipsis between head/tail; fmtAmount leans on Intl.NumberFormat en-US with 2 fraction digits + space-joined currency label default USDC; fmtCompact uses Intl compact notation with 1 fraction digit), and src/lib/prng.ts (mulberry32 — `state >>>= 0` seeding, Math.imul bit-mixing, normalized by 2^32). Prng interface exports Prng type for downstream imports. Tests: format.test.ts covers 3 functions × 3 assertions (9 total); prng.test.ts covers range validity, determinism across two instances of same seed, different seeds produce different values, range() bounds, pick() determinism + empty-array throw. All 15 tests pass. Typecheck, lint, build all clean; landing still 102 kB first-load.

Focus state uses 2px forest outline with 2px offset on every primitive
Primitives render a demo route or Storybook-like test page for visual verification

userStories.notes: Authored 6 primitives in src/components/ui/: button.tsx (cva-driven primary/ghost/link × sm/md/lg, radius-3, forest-hover on primary, ink-outline on ghost, underline on link), input.tsx (radius-2 border-subtle, hover:border-border, focus:border-forest), select.tsx (native <select> appearance-none + absolute chevron SVG), slider.tsx (native range + .zks-slider pseudo-element styling added to globals.css for webkit/moz track+thumb), badge.tsx (default/success/warning/danger/info/forest variants using mint/warning-bg/danger-bg/info-bg tokens), tabs.tsx (controlled+uncontrolled via useId-scoped Context; TabsList/TabsTrigger/TabsContent with aria-selected/controls, roving tabindex, Arrow/Home/End keyboard nav). All focus-visible states emit 2px forest outline + 2px offset. Demo route src/app/ui/page.tsx showcases every variant and size. Build passes (12 kB first-load on /ui), lint clean, 15 tests still pass, typecheck green. Browser verification via curl confirmed 200 + rendered labels + ARIA roles + token classes (agent-browser CLI not installed on this machine, so no screenshot).

userStories.title: StatusPill + StatCard + TransactionRow primitives
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable dashboard primitives so tables and headers share one visual language.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/components/dashboard/status-pill.tsx renders Iconoir glyph + label in one of: verified (mint bg, forest text, Check glyph), blocked (danger-bg, danger-text, Xmark), warning (warning-bg, warning-text, Warning triangle), info (info-bg, info-text, InfoCircle), test (surface-deep, muted, Sparks)
StatusPill never renders a bare colored dot — always Iconoir + label
frontend/src/components/dashboard/stat-card.tsx renders display-m number + mono-l sub-label on surface with border-subtle

frontend/src/components/dashboard/transaction-row.tsx renders a table row with 2px left vertical rule (emerald|danger-text|muted by status), columns Time/Wallet/Issuer/Status pill/Amount/Jurisdiction/Tx, hover reveals View proof bytes ▾
Stories/demo route shows all primitives
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: /dashboard/transactions — live feed (Tier A)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the default dashboard to be a live feed of settlement events with stat cards, filters, and a pseudo-realtime ticker.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/dashboard/transactions/page.tsx renders page subtitle 'Live feed of settlement events. Every row carries a proof; every proof is replayable six months from now.'
4 stat cards at top: 1,847 proofs verified (+12% vs yesterday), 23 blocked (1.2% rejection rate), 4.7s avg proving (p95 6.2s), $0.00091 avg verify cost (Devnet)
Filter bar: toggles All | Verified | Blocked + date range picker (visual only)
Table uses TransactionRow; columns align with component
Pseudo-realtime: setInterval pushes a new event every 3-8s (deterministic by seed, URL ?seed=N override), new row fades in, DOM capped at 100 rows
Cleanup setInterval on route change (no zombie timers)
Zero console errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: /dashboard/counterparties — issuer list (Tier A)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a table of registered issuers showing their Merkle roots, users, last update, and status.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/dashboard/counterparties/page.tsx renders page subtitle 'Issuers that have published a Merkle root your policies trust.'
Table columns: Name, Pubkey, Merkle root, Users, Last update, Status pill
6 rows from ISSUERS (Persona, Sumsub, Onfido, Jumio, Veriff, MockKYC) with status pills per design: Check+Active forest, Warning triangle+Stale, Sparks+Test
CTA top right: 'Register issuer →' forest button opens a modal scaffold with 'Available in private beta' footer (click closes modal)
All addresses rendered in Berkeley Mono truncated via format.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: /dashboard/audit-log — exportable history (Tier A)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a paginated audit log of every attestation with filters and export buttons (mock).
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/dashboard/audit-log/page.tsx renders page subtitle 'Every attestation, exportable on request.'
Filter bar: date range, issuer dropdown, status, jurisdiction
Table columns: Time, Wallet, Issuer, Status pill, Amount, Jurisdiction, Proof hash, Block, Slot, CU consumed, Tx
Export bar top right: Export CSV, Export JSON, Webhook digest (ghost buttons). Click toasts 'Available in private beta · request access ↗'
Pagination uses Berkeley Mono '← 1 2 3 ... 47 →'
Footer line: 'Showing 50 of 23,481 attestations · Last 30 days'
Zero console errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: /dashboard/billing — usage + invoices (Tier A)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my current tier, 30-day usage chart, and last 3 invoices.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/dashboard/billing/page.tsx renders page subtitle 'Pay for what you prove.'

Three stacked cards: Current tier (Startup · 50,000 proofs/mo · $0.05/proof), Used this month 18,432 (37%) with forest progress bar; Usage chart (30-day line chart, mono axis labels in muted, line in forest, no fill, hairline grid); Invoices table (3 rows, Download PDF ghost buttons toasting 'Available in private beta')
Chart built with SVG or a lightweight lib; no client-heavy chart lib
Progress bar and chart colors use forest and border-subtle tokens only
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: TierBScaffold + 4 scaffolded routes
userStories.description: As a user, I want attestations, policies, api-keys, and team routes to render the same chrome with a clean coming-soon empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/components/dashboard/tier-b-scaffold.tsx accepts {icon, title, body} and renders a centered empty state: 64px Iconoir glyph in ghost + display-m headline + one-line stone body + 'Request access ↗' ghost button

frontend/src/app/dashboard/attestations/page.tsx uses TierBScaffold with Check glyph, 'Attestation explorer · coming soon', 'Filter, search, and inspect every ComplianceAttestation. Available to private-beta participants.'
frontend/src/app/dashboard/policies/page.tsx uses TierBScaffold with Page glyph and policies copy
frontend/src/app/dashboard/api-keys/page.tsx uses TierBScaffold with Key glyph and api keys copy
frontend/src/app/dashboard/team/page.tsx uses TierBScaffold with Group glyph and team copy
All 4 routes share the Dashboard layout (sidebar + topbar + page title + subtitle)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Mobile fallbacks audit + fixes
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the landing to remain readable and usable without jank.
acceptanceCriteria: Viewport <768px: Veil canvas swaps to a static SVG placeholder
Two Realities pin disabled; cards stack vertically with sequential fade-in
Dashboard sidebar collapses to a hamburger drawer (tap to open/close, focus trap active when open)
Section vertical rhythm reduced to 96px per spec
Outer page gutter 20px per spec
All CTAs remain full-width tappable (min 44x44px hit area)
Lighthouse mobile: Performance ≥ 85, A11y ≥ 95, SEO ≥ 95
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (emulate iPhone 15 viewport)

userStories.title: Accessibility pass
userStories.description: As a keyboard or assistive-tech user, I need every interactive surface reachable, announced, and reduced-motion aware.
acceptanceCriteria: Skip-to-content link focusable on Tab at the top of every page
All buttons/inputs/links have 2px forest focus outline with 2px offset
Demo section aria-live='polite' announces proof-generation state changes
Dashboard sidebar has aria-current='page' on active route; all nav links have visible label (not icon only)
prefers-reduced-motion disables GSAP pins, Three.js animation loop, and motion fades (verified via CDP override)
Contrast: ink on canvas ≥ 12:1, forest on canvas ≥ 10:1 (verified via axe or manual)
axe-core or Lighthouse a11y ≥ 95 on landing AND /dashboard/transactions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: OG image route + favicon + SEO metadata
userStories.description: As a link share, I want a proper OG preview and favicon so the site looks intentional everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/og/route.tsx exports GET returning an ImageResponse (1200×630, forest bg, ZKSettle Georgia 96px + subline 'Compliance-grade rails for stablecoin settlement.' 36px)
frontend/public/favicon.svg exists as the Seal-only variant
metadata.openGraph.images in layout.tsx points to /og
metadata.icons.icon points to /favicon.svg
metadata.twitter.card = 'summary_large_image', title, description, images /og
Visiting /og renders a valid 1200×630 image in the browser
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (navigate to /og)

userStories.title: Lighthouse pass + performance fixes
userStories.description: As a user, I want the landing and dashboard to meet the spec's performance budget.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm build produces a first-load JS on landing ≤ 220KB gzipped (verified via build output)
pnpm build produces a first-load JS on /dashboard/transactions ≤ 120KB gzipped
next.config.ts has experimental.optimizePackageImports including 'iconoir-react'
Three.js imports are tree-shaken (named imports, not import * as THREE)
Lighthouse run on http://localhost:3000 (after pnpm build && pnpm start): Performance ≥ 85, A11y ≥ 95, SEO ≥ 95, LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms
Lighthouse run on http://localhost:3000/dashboard/transactions: Performance ≥ 85, A11y ≥ 95, SEO ≥ 95
Lighthouse HTML reports saved to frontend/lighthouse-landing.html and frontend/lighthouse-dashboard.html
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Smoke test every flow end-to-end
userStories.description: As a QA, I want a documented manual smoke that proves every page, CTA, and animation works at least in Chromium.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing loads at / with no console errors; hero canvas renders or static fallback activates
Scroll through all 10 sections — fades and Two Realities pin work (no stuck pins)
Demo flow: click Generate proof, see 4-step animation, submit-to-devnet morph, Solscan link opens in new tab
Try expired credential toggle → [3/4] fails in danger-text
/dashboard redirects to /dashboard/transactions
Sidebar navigation to each of 8 routes works without console errors
Cmd-K visual hint present (non-functional)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (run the full flow)

userStories.title: Finalize README for submission
userStories.description: As a Colosseum judge, I want a clear README covering stack, run, build, test, deploy, and design system links.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/README.md contains: project summary, Stack section (Next.js 15, React 19, TS strict, Tailwind v4, Three.js, GSAP, motion, Shiki, Iconoir, Geist + Berkeley Mono fallback)
Run section: pnpm install + pnpm dev
Build section: pnpm build + pnpm start
Test section: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint
Deploy section: Vercel auto-deploy from main plus fallback URL note
Design system section: links to ../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-zksettle-landing-design.md and the canonical design board URL
No broken relative links
Typecheck passes
